fix: add schedule to improve.yml to pick up approved-for-work issues

GitHub Actions does not trigger workflows from label events caused by
other Actions (GITHUB_TOKEN restriction). Added daily 10am UTC schedule
(1hr after review at 9am) to poll for approved-for-work issues.

Also supports workflow_dispatch for manual triggering.
Retains label event trigger for cases where a human manually labels.
Idempotent: skips issues already marked in-progress.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Pre-release fixes: CI benchmark path, template version, CHANGELOG, README

- ci.yml: fix benchmark artifact path — was 'SageTUI.Benchmarks-report-full-compressed.json'
  (assembly name), now 'SageTUI.Benchmarks.BufferDiffBenchmarks-report-full-compressed.json'
  (correct BenchmarkDotNet class-based naming). Benchmark CI has never worked before this fix.
- Benchmarks/Program.fs: replace four BenchmarkRunner.Run<T>() calls with
  BenchmarkSwitcher.FromAssembly(...).Run(argv) — correct architecture for CLI invocation
- templates/sagetui-app/SageTuiApp.fsproj: Version 0.1.0 -> 0.9.0 (0.1.0 never existed
  on NuGet.org — scaffolded apps would fail to restore on day one)
- SageTUI.Tests/TemplateSmokeTests.fs: update version assertion to match 0.9.0
- CHANGELOG.md: add '## [0.9.0] - 2025-01-01' header; fix orphaned sentence fragment
  on line 103 (benchmark-regression job note was detached from its parent list item)
- README.md: add ## Testing section (TestHarness + TuiExpect API examples);
  fix 'Package version: 0.1.0' -> '0.9.0'
- SageTUI.Library.fsproj: update PackageReleaseNotes — 2,544 -> 2,551 tests,
  add drain overflow protection and TestHarness click simulation to notes
- gh-pages branch created (separate commit 4c5fe2e) to break the benchmark
  bootstrap deadlock (benchmark-regression job could never succeed without a baseline)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reject accumulator updates when source unit changes mid-session

Prevents data corruption if a source entity changes unit_of_measurement
while a session is active. Updates are silently dropped until the unit
matches the accumulator's locked unit again.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: suppress Azure Identity logging during token acquisition

The ChainedTokenCredential tries multiple credential providers in
sequence and logs [ERROR] for each one that fails. These are expected
failures — not all providers are available in all environments.

In Maven, SLF4J routes through Maven's SimpleLogger to System.out.
When tools like CMake capture Maven's stdout (e.g., via
execute_process with 'mvn help:evaluate -DforceStdout'), these error
messages pollute the output and break builds.

Fix by temporarily setting the SLF4J SimpleLogger log level for
com.azure.identity to 'off' during token acquisition, restoring
the previous value afterward.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update scripts/clean.sh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix race condition in server stdin forwarding causing intermittent CI failures (#12788)

* Initial plan

* Fix race condition in server stdin forwarding

Move Unix.clear_nonblock into SocketRequest.read before spawning the
stdin forwarding thread. Previously it was called after read returned,
creating a window where the forwarding thread's Unix.recv could hit
EWOULDBLOCK on the still-non-blocking socket, causing it to exit
prematurely and close the stdin pipe before all data was forwarded.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

all 5 handler files:

- core.rs: 21 map_err + 11 arg extractions → helpers
- automation.rs: 26 map_err + 9 arg extractions → helpers
- network.rs: 24 map_err + 9 arg extractions → helpers
- dom.rs: 14 map_err + 12 arg extractions → helpers
- media.rs: 6 map_err → cdp_err

Total: 91 map_err(|e| e.to_string()) → map_err(cdp_err)
       41 inline arg extraction chains → helper calls

commit.message:
fix: formatCurrency now expects integer cents, not dollars

All money values are stored as integer cents in the database, but
formatCurrency() was treating its input as dollars — causing summaries,
charts, and totals to display 100x too large.

- Updated formatCurrency() to divide by 100 internally
- Removed redundant / 100 in billFieldFormatters and BillPaidActivityAccordion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: clean label state machine across all workflows

Label state machine:
  game-improvement (only)          → unreviewed, picked up by daily review
  + approved-for-work              → triggers improve.yml (transient)
  + in-progress (approved removed) → being implemented, review skips
  closed                           → done or rejected

improve.yml:
- Idempotency check: skip if already in-progress
- On assign: add in-progress, remove approved-for-work (prevents re-trigger)

auto-merge.yml:
- After merge: close all linked issues with comment

review-improvements.md:
- Skip issues already labelled approved-for-work or in-progress

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: shared song analysis cache (server-side SQLite)

Add a server-side cache for DJ song analysis results (BPM, beat grid,
key, genre, waveform peaks) keyed by SHA-256 file hash. When any user
analyzes a song, results are stored on the server so all users benefit.

Storage is binary-packed for minimal footprint (~5KB/song):
- Beats/downbeats: delta-encoded Uint16 (ms between events)
- Waveform peaks: Uint8 quantized (0-255 from 0.0-1.0)
- Frequency peaks: 3×N Uint8 bands (low/mid/high)

Pipeline integration:
- On load: check server cache after local IndexedDB miss
- After analysis: fire-and-forget POST to populate server cache
- Server lookups have 3s timeout so they never block playback

New API endpoints:
- GET  /api/analysis/lookup/:hash — retrieve cached analysis
- POST /api/analysis/cache — store analysis results
- GET  /api/analysis/status — cache statistics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

NNlib activation function rules (#1043)

* NNlib activation function rules

* tight dispatces

* bump vers

* stability

* loose dispatch to accomodate CUDAArrays

* type stability

* fin

* futureproof fallback, permutation index stability.

* fix

* fix for GPU

* handle Inf instability

* Update ext/MooncakeNNlibExt.jl

Co-authored-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Astitva Aggarwal <84859349+AstitvaAggarwal@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use arrayify for Adjoint/Transpose in NNlib rrules (#1048)

- Add arrayify methods for Adjoint and Transpose in blas.jl, so
  that arrayify(x, dx) correctly wraps the tangent in adjoint/transpose,
  making dx .+= g equivalent to accumulating g' or transpose(g) into
  the parent tangent via Julia's broadcast setindex! semantics.

- Remove the _accum_fdata! helper and all its dispatch overloads from
  MooncakeNNlibExt. Each pullback now calls arrayify(x) directly on the
  CoDual and destructures: _, dx = arrayify(x); dx .+= g.

- Update tests to exercise arrayify directly for Adjoint/Transpose
  accumulation instead of testing the removed helper.

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Widen SupportedArray to include Adjoint/Transpose (#1049)

* refactor: use arrayify for Adjoint/Transpose in NNlib rrules

- Add arrayify methods for Adjoint and Transpose in blas.jl, so
  that arrayify(x, dx) correctly wraps the tangent in adjoint/transpose,
  making dx .+= g equivalent to accumulating g' or transpose(g) into
  the parent tangent via Julia's broadcast setindex! semantics.

- Remove the _accum_fdata! helper and all its dispatch overloads from
  MooncakeNNlibExt. Each pullback now calls arrayify(x) directly on the
  CoDual and destructures: _, dx = arrayify(x); dx .+= g.

- Update tests to exercise arrayify directly for Adjoint/Transpose
  accumulation instead of testing the removed helper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Widen SupportedArray to include Adjoint/Transpose of CPU/GPU arrays

   - Redefine SupportedArray{P} as a 1-parameter union covering Array{P},
     AbstractGPUArray{P}, and their Adjoint/Transpose wrappers
   - Inline Union{Array{P,3},AbstractGPUArray{P,3}} for batched_mul (which
     requires N=3 and does not support Adjoint/Transpose)
   - Restore Adjoint/Transpose test cases for softmax, logsoftmax, logsumexp
   - Change all @is_primitive and rrulegit diff signatures from AbstractArray{T}
     to SupportedArray{T}
   - Remove now-unnecessary _xp/xp collect workaround in logsumexp_kw
   - Drop LinearAlgebra: Adjoint, Transpose import (no longer needed)

   Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid PermutedDimsArray type instability in logsumexp kw rrule

On Julia <= 1.11, maximum(x::Adjoint/Transpose; dims, init) routes through
LinearAlgebra.mapreducedim! -> switch_dim12 -> PermutedDimsArray, leaving
type parameters unresolved and causing JET type-stability failures.

Fix: introduce _maximum helper that collects CPU-backed Adjoint/Transpose
wrappers to a plain Array before calling maximum. Dispatches via
x::Tx where Tx<:Union{Adjoint{T,A},Transpose{T,A}} with A<:Array{T}
so the more-specific method is selected for CPU wrappers; all other
SupportedArray types (plain Array, GPU arrays, GPU-backed wrappers) fall
through to the generic method which calls maximum directly.

The entire workaround is version-gated under @static if VERSION < v"1.12"
where the issue was fixed upstream.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace index with id in actions routes (#36842)

This PR migrates the web Actions run/job routes from index-based
`runIndex` or `jobIndex` to database IDs.

**⚠️ BREAKING ⚠️**: Existing saved links/bookmarks that use the old
index-based URLs will no longer resolve after this change.

Improvements of this change:
- Previously, `jobIndex` depended on list order, making it hard to
locate a specific job. Using `jobID` provides stable addressing.
- Web routes now align with API, which already use IDs.
- Behavior is closer to GitHub, which exposes run/job IDs in URLs.
- Provides a cleaner base for future features without relying on list
order.
- #36388 this PR improves the support for reusable workflows. If a job
uses a reusable workflow, it may contain multiple child jobs, which
makes relying on job index to locate a job much more complicated

---------

Signed-off-by: Zettat123 <zettat123@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: initial architecture documentation

Port design documentation from punt-labs/dungeon (pure-prompt v1).
cryptd is the Go game engine daemon that supersedes the Node.js MCP
server and pure-prompt engine in dungeon.

- DESIGN.md: ADRs DES-001 through DES-022; evolved architecture
- docs/architecture.tex/pdf: 19-page technical architecture spec
- docs/plan.md: architecture evolution plan with MCP API and schemas
- docs/testing.md: test pyramid design; fakes; CI configuration
- docs/build-plan.md: 14-milestone development build plan
- docs/review.md: compliance review against punt-kit standards
- prfaq.tex/bib/pdf: PR/FAQ document
- README.md: project overview and documentation index

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pdf-ocr): chunked PDFs producing empty OCR results (#169)

* Initial plan

* fix(pdf-ocr): save chunks with useObjectStreams:false and validate PDF header

- Use `{ useObjectStreams: false }` when saving PDF chunks with pdf-lib, producing
  PDF 1.4-compatible output (no cross-reference streams) that is more broadly
  supported by OCR engines such as Mistral OCR via OpenRouter.
- Add `%PDF-` magic-byte header validation immediately after each chunk is saved.
  If pdf-lib fails to produce a valid PDF, a clear error is thrown showing the
  chunk number and page range, instead of sending malformed bytes to the OCR API
  and receiving an opaque empty-content response.
- Update existing multi-chunk test: mock save() to return bytes starting with
  `%PDF-1.4` so the new header validation passes, and assert that save() is
  called with `{ useObjectStreams: false }`.
- Add new test: invalid chunk bytes (all zeros, no PDF header) produce a
  descriptive error message identifying which chunk failed.

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>
